value.title: Enable enterprise authentication, authorization, and auditability for APMA
value.description:
## Summary
Deliver secure, enterprise-grade access control for APMA using Microsoft Entra ID, role-based authorization, and full audit trails across API and UI.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce onboarding time for new teams by providing standard SSO and least-privilege roles
- Meet enterprise security/compliance expectations (auditability, access reviews, incident readiness)

## Scope (In)
- Entra ID auth (OIDC) for UI and API
- API authorization policies for core operations (create session, generate items, publish)
- Audit logging for security-relevant events (auth, publish, configuration changes)
- Secretless access patterns (Managed Identity where applicable)

## Scope (Out)
- Custom identity provider support
- Fine-grained row-level authorization beyond project scoping

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a user is authenticated via Entra ID, when they access the UI, then they can start a session without manual token handling
- Given a user lacks the required role, when they attempt to publish work items, then the API responds with 403 and logs an authorization audit event
- Given any publish action succeeds or fails, when the workflow completes, then an audit record is produced with correlation IDs

## Definition of Done
- Threat model updated for auth flows and token handling
- Security logging includes correlationId, userId (or subject), action, outcome
- Unit/integration tests cover allow/deny paths for critical endpoints
- Documentation includes roles, permissions, and operational runbook for access issues

## Observability
- Emit trace spans for authentication, authorization, and publish operations
- Dashboard includes auth failure rate and publish failure rate

value.title: Establish observability, reliability, and incident readiness for APMA
value.description:
## Summary
Implement an observability baseline (logs, metrics, traces) and operational practices (runbook, alerting) so APMA can be supported in production with confidence.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce MTTR by making failures diagnosable with correlated telemetry
- Prevent regressions by tracking latency/error budgets for core operations
- Enable safe operations with documented incident playbooks

## Success Metrics
- 95% of generation requests have end-to-end trace coverage
- Key operations (session creation, generation, publish) expose latency (p95) and failure rate metrics
- On-call can identify likely root cause using a runbook within 15 minutes

## Scope (In)
- OpenTelemetry instrumentation in API + background workflows
- Correlation IDs propagated across API, agent calls, and search calls
- Structured logging with safe redaction and consistent fields
- Metrics for latency, error rate, retry/backoff behavior, and dependency health
- Alerting guidance for elevated error rates and latency regressions
- Runbook entries for common failures (AI throttling, search unavailable, auth issues)

## Scope (Out)
- Custom APM vendor dashboards beyond baseline templates
- Advanced anomaly detection / ML-driven alerting
- Organization-wide SIEM integration specifics (covered by platform/security programs)

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a user starts a generation run, when events stream via SSE, then logs and traces share a correlationId end-to-end
- Given an error occurs calling an external service, when the API returns a non-200, then logs include safe diagnostics (no secrets) and a dependency span is captured
- Given latency regresses for a core operation, when p95 exceeds the threshold, then an alert is triggered with links to traces and recent deployments

## Observability
- Traces: API request spans; dependency spans for AI/Search/DevOps; workflow stage spans
- Metrics: request rate, error rate, p50/p95 latency, retries, 429 rate, timeouts, circuit-breaker opens

## Definition of Done
- Traces include dependency spans for Foundry and Search calls
- Error logs are structured, searchable, and redact sensitive data
- Runbook includes investigation steps and mitigation playbooks
- Smoke tests verify telemetry is emitted in dev and at least one non-dev environment

value.title: Provide RAG-backed work item generation using curated templates and golden examples
value.description:
## Summary
Integrate Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) into generator agents so they consistently produce enterprise-ready work items aligned to organizational standards.

## Functional Overview
- Generator agents retrieve context from approved sources before each invocation
- Context includes golden examples, work item templates, and domain standards
- Retrieved content is cited or referenced to support reviewer trust

## Non-Functional Requirements
- Retrieval must be deterministic (always executed before generation)
- No secrets in prompts; only approved content
- Retrieval must be scoped by work item type where relevant

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a request to generate a Feature, when generation runs, then at least 1 template and up to 3 golden examples are retrieved when available
- Given no relevant context is found, when generation runs, then output still meets minimum quality standards without placeholder text

## Definition of Done
- RAG integration is implemented via AIContextProviderFactory with TextSearchProvider
- Integration test validates that search results flow into the agent invocation
- Logging includes which sources were retrieved (IDs only, not full content)

value.title: Implement quality gates for generated work items (schema + quality warnings)
value.description:
## Summary
Ensure generated work items meet schema requirements and recommended quality standards before presenting them to the user.

## Scope
- Hard gate: validates required fields and structured output schema
- Soft gate: warns on weak titles, short descriptions, missing AC/DoD coverage

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a generated PBI has fewer than 2 acceptance criteria, when validation runs, then a warning is recorded and shown to the user
- Given a generated item is missing required fields, when validation runs, then the pipeline blocks and retries (once) with injected validation feedback

## Definition of Done
- Gate results persist in session state and are visible in UI
- Unit tests cover representative failures and warning conditions
- Gates do not modify user content; they only validate and annotate

value.title: Wire Epic Planner agent to use RAG context with TextSearchProvider
value.description:
As a product manager, I want the Epic Planner agent to retrieve approved context before generating epics, so that outputs match our enterprise standards and reduce reviewer rework.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given an epic generation request, when the agent runs, then it retrieves relevant golden examples and templates before invoking the model
- Given retrieved items exist, when the agent returns an output, then it reflects the style and structure of the retrieved examples (clear scope, measurable outcomes)
- Given the search service is unavailable, when generation runs, then the system degrades gracefully and logs a warning without failing the request

### Definition of Done
- Agent uses AIContextProviderFactory with TextSearchProvider in BeforeAIInvoke mode
- Structured output schema enforcement remains enabled
- Unit test covers search delegate invocation and fallback behavior

value.title: Create Azure AI Search indexes for golden examples, templates, feedback, and domain context
value.description:
As a platform engineer, I want APMA to automatically create or update its Azure AI Search indexes on startup, so that environments can be provisioned consistently and RAG retrieval works without manual setup.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the search service exists, when the initializer runs, then required indexes are created/updated idempotently
- Given an index already exists, when the initializer runs, then schema changes are applied safely
- Given initialization fails, when the API starts, then the failure is observable (logs + health check details)

### Definition of Done
- Index schemas match the retrieval queries used by the application
- Logging includes index names and operation outcomes
- Basic smoke test verifies indexes exist after startup

value.title: Enable governed publishing to Azure DevOps with preview, traceability, and rollback
value.description:
## Summary
Allow users to publish generated epics/features/PBIs to Azure DevOps with safeguards: preview, approvals, traceability, and a clear rollback strategy.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce time from idea to actionable backlog while maintaining governance
- Improve auditability and reduce accidental changes to ADO projects
- Increase reviewer confidence with clear diffs and publish summaries

## Success Metrics
- 100% of publish operations produce a publish summary with correlationId and created work item IDs
- <1% of publish attempts require manual cleanup
- Publish preview is generated in <10s for typical sessions

## Scope (In)
- Publish preview that shows diffs and hierarchy (Epic → Feature → PBI)
- Publishing via a service identity with least privilege
- Correlation between APMA draft IDs and ADO work item IDs
- Idempotency and safe retry strategy (detect duplicates; avoid double-creates)
- Rollback guidance (delete/revert tags; operational playbook)

## Scope (Out)
- Bi-directional sync (updates flowing back from ADO into APMA)
- Complex approval workflows integrated with external GRC tooling
- Bulk edits of existing ADO work items outside the current publish session

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a user requests a publish preview, when preview is generated, then it includes work item count, hierarchy, and key field diffs
- Given a publish operation runs, when it completes, then it records a publish summary with created IDs and correlationId
- Given publishing fails partway, when the workflow ends, then it produces a recovery plan (what was created, what remains, and recommended cleanup)

## Observability
- Traces: publish preview, create item, link item, retry spans
- Metrics: publish success rate, failure rate by step, retries, average publish duration

## Definition of Done
- Integration tests validate create + link operations
- Operational runbook includes rollback guidance and common failure modes
- Security review confirms least-privilege scopes and no PAT leakage
- UI surfaces publish summaries and failure recovery instructions

value.title: Reduce time-to-triage for a secure case workflow portal (queues, case summary, actions)
value.description:
## Summary
Deliver a workflow-first experience that helps users quickly find the right case, understand its status and next action, and record follow-up—without exposing sensitive data unnecessarily.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce time spent locating and interpreting case context
- Increase throughput by standardizing triage steps and minimizing back-and-forth
- Improve audit readiness with consistent action capture

## Success Metrics
- Reduce median time from queue open → first action recorded by 30%
- Reduce wrong-case opens by 50% (proxy: back navigations within 15s after open)
- Increase action completeness to >90% (cases with a disposition or follow-up note when required)

## Scope (In)
- Work queues with prioritization, filtering (status/age/owner), and safe empty/error states
- Case summary with status, timeline, assigned owner, and next-action guidance
- Standard actions: assign/reassign, disposition tags, add notes, request info
- Immutable activity history surfaced in UI (audit-backed)

## Scope (Out)
- Automated decisioning / rules engines
- Bulk administrative overrides that modify authoritative records

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a user opens a queue, when they filter and select a case, then the case summary loads with clear next-action guidance
- Given a user takes an action (assign, disposition, note), when saved, then the action is visible in the case history and reflected back in the queue

## Observability
- Metrics: time-to-first-action, wrong-case-open proxy, action completion rate
- Traces: queue load, case load, action save

## Definition of Done
- UX flows validated with representative users
- Key actions emit telemetry events with correlationId
- Permission-denied and partial-data states are handled safely

value.title: Enable queue-based triage with assignment, ownership, and aging controls
value.description:
## Summary
Provide operational queues that let triage users prioritize work by status, age, and ownership, and perform safe assignment/routing actions with full auditability.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce backlog aging by making ownership and priority visible
- Improve throughput by enabling efficient queue management

## Success Metrics
- Reduce items over aging threshold by 20%
- Increase % of queue items with clear owner to 95%

## Scope (In)
- Queue views with filters (status, age, owner, priority) and sorting
- Assignment/reassignment actions with permission checks
- Visual aging indicators and SLA/threshold highlighting
- Bulk-safe operations limited to low-risk actions (e.g., assign)

## Scope (Out)
- Complex workflow engines
- High-risk bulk updates to authoritative records

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a queue is loaded, when a user filters by age and owner, then results update quickly and predictably
- Given a user reassigns an item, when saved, then ownership updates in the queue and an audit event is recorded

## Observability
- Metrics: queue load latency, assignment success rate, aging distribution

## Definition of Done
- Queues handle empty/no-result/error states
- Assignment actions are audited and permission-gated

value.title: Provide case summary views with next-action guidance and audit-ready history
value.description:
## Summary
Consolidate essential case context into a single summary view and guide users toward the next safe action, while ensuring all key interactions are auditable and least-privilege.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce time spent assembling context across screens/tools
- Improve follow-up consistency and audit readiness

## Success Metrics
- Reduce time-to-understand-case status by 30% (proxy: time-to-first-scroll + time-to-action)
- Increase next-action completion rate by 25%

## Scope (In)
- Case overview: status, timeline, ownership, key flags
- Next-action panel driven by case state (informational, not automated adjudication)
- Activity history surfaced from immutable audit events
- PII minimization patterns (masking, progressive disclosure by role)

## Scope (Out)
- Full adjudication decision logic
- Free-form data access outside role entitlements

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a user opens a case, when the summary loads, then they can identify status, owner, and next action without navigating elsewhere
- Given an action is taken, when saved, then the history reflects it with actor, timestamp, and outcome

## Observability
- Metrics: case load latency, action rate per case, masking/unmasking usage (if applicable)

## Definition of Done
- Summary view meets accessibility and safe-data guidelines
- History is immutable and consistent with audit events

value.title: Enable alerts triage with acknowledgement, case linking, and follow-up completeness
value.description:
## Summary
Provide an alerts surface that allows users to acknowledge alerts, link them to cases, and record follow-up actions consistently so nothing falls through the cracks.

## Business Outcomes
- Improve alert handling completeness and reduce missed follow-ups
- Increase traceability between alerts and case actions

## Success Metrics
- 90% of alerts acknowledged within SLA window
- 80% of alerts have a recorded follow-up action when required

## Scope (In)
- Alerts list with filtering and safe defaults
- Acknowledge/unacknowledge with audit events
- Link alerts to cases (or mark as unlinked with reason)
- Follow-up actions: notes, dispositions, routing

## Scope (Out)
- Automated alert resolution
- Complex correlation/triangulation engines

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given an alert is visible, when acknowledged, then the acknowledgement is recorded and visible in both alert and case history (if linked)
- Given follow-up is required, when recorded, then metrics reflect completeness and the case timeline updates

## Observability
- Metrics: acknowledgement rate, follow-up completion rate, unlinked alert rate

## Definition of Done
- Alerts UX handles empty/no-result/error states
- All key actions are audited

value.title: Provide operational visibility for workload health (counts, aging, throughput) with safe exports
value.description:
## Summary
Deliver lightweight operational reporting that helps leaders and operators understand workload health—without exposing sensitive data—and supports basic exports for downstream workflows.

## Business Outcomes
- Improve visibility into bottlenecks and aging items
- Support operational reporting needs without building a full BI platform

## Success Metrics
- Dashboard p95 load ≤ 3s for standard ranges
- Metrics definitions documented and used consistently

## Scope (In)
- Core metrics: counts by status, aging distribution, throughput over time
- Segmentation by owner/team/project (as applicable)
- Safe CSV exports with role-based field selection
- Metric definitions glossary

## Scope (Out)
- Self-service report builder
- Predictive analytics

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given a user opens the dashboard, when viewing a standard range, then metrics render quickly with clear definitions
- Given a user exports a report, when generated, then export respects RBAC and excludes restricted fields

## Observability
- Metrics: dashboard latency, export success rate, most-used filters

## Definition of Done
- Exports are permission-gated and audited
- Metric definitions are documented

value.title: Stream generation progress to the UI using SSE with correlation IDs
value.description:
## Summary
Provide real-time progress updates during generation via Server-Sent Events (SSE) so users can understand what the system is doing and troubleshoot when needed.

## Functional Requirements
- SSE endpoint streams workflow stage events (retrieve, generate, validate, publish)
- Events include correlationId and timestamps
- UI renders progress and error states without polling

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a generation is in progress, when a user opens the session, then they see streaming progress within 2 seconds
- Given an error occurs, when an SSE error event is emitted, then the UI shows a human-readable message and keeps partial results

## Definition of Done
- SSE messages are versioned and documented
- Telemetry captures event counts and stage latency
- Tests cover connect/disconnect and error scenarios

value.title: Implement resilience patterns for external dependencies (AI, Search, DevOps)
value.description:
## Summary
Standardize retry/backoff, timeouts, and fallback behaviors for calls to external services to improve reliability under throttling and transient failures.

## Scope
- Respect Retry-After and implement exponential backoff on 429
- Use timeouts and cancellation tokens consistently
- Provide graceful degradation when context retrieval fails

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given AI services return 429, when the system retries, then it uses Retry-After when provided and logs the backoff decision
- Given Search is unavailable, when generation runs, then the workflow continues with a warning and no crash

## Definition of Done
- Centralized policy utilities (reused across integrations)
- Tests simulate 429/503 conditions
- Logs include dependency name, status code, and correlationId

value.title: Implement Retry-After aware backoff for AI throttling responses
value.description:
As a platform engineer, I want agent calls to honor 429 Retry-After headers and apply exponential backoff, so that APMA remains reliable under load and avoids cascading failures.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given an AI call returns 429 with Retry-After, when retrying, then the delay matches Retry-After within a small jitter
- Given 429 occurs without Retry-After, when retrying, then backoff uses exponential strategy with a max delay
- Given retries exceed the configured limit, when the operation fails, then the user sees a clear error and the failure is logged with correlationId

### Definition of Done
- Implementation is cancelable via CancellationToken
- Logs redact any sensitive request data
- Unit tests cover both Retry-After and no-header cases

value.title: Add structured logging with redaction and end-to-end correlation
value.description:
As an operator, I want logs to be structured, correlated, and safe, so that I can troubleshoot issues without exposing secrets or prompt content.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given a request is processed, when logs are emitted, then they include correlationId, sessionId (if available), and outcome
- Given an exception occurs, when it is logged, then it includes dependency context and a safe message (no secrets)

### Definition of Done
- Logging fields are consistent across API, workflow, and integration layers
- Redaction rules cover tokens, keys, and any user-provided secrets
- Tests validate that known secret patterns are removed

value.title: Seed enterprise RAG content (golden examples, templates, domain standards) into Search
value.description:
As a platform engineer, I want initial RAG content seeded into Azure AI Search, so that generator agents can produce enterprise-ready outputs from day one.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given seed data exists in source control, when seeding runs, then golden examples, templates, feedback patterns, and domain context are uploaded to their indexes
- Given seeding is run multiple times, when it re-runs, then documents are merged or overwritten idempotently
- Given some docs fail, when seeding completes, then it reports failures with document IDs and continues processing others

### Definition of Done
- Seed content is curated for enterprise standards (security, observability, compliance)
- Seeding supports local dev and CI
- Basic verification query returns expected seeded docs

value.title: Establish architecture, infrastructure, and foundational capabilities for [Application Name]
value.description:
## Summary
Define and implement the foundational technical architecture, infrastructure, and cross-cutting capabilities required to build [Application Name]. This epic unblocks all feature development by establishing the technical foundation.

## Business Outcomes
- Reduce technical debt by making deliberate architecture decisions upfront
- Enable parallel feature development with clear API contracts and data models
- Ensure production readiness from day one with proper DevOps practices

## Scope (In)
- **Architecture & Design**: System architecture, ERD/data models, API contracts, tech stack selection
- **Infrastructure & DevOps**: CI/CD pipelines, environment provisioning (dev/staging/prod), IaC templates
- **Authentication & Authorization**: Identity provider integration, user roles, permission model
- **Observability Baseline**: Structured logging, distributed tracing, health checks, basic alerting
- **Security Baseline**: Secret management, network security, vulnerability scanning

## Scope (Out)
- Feature-specific business logic (covered by other epics)
- Advanced analytics/reporting infrastructure (unless core to MVP)
- Performance optimization beyond baseline (covered in later iterations)

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given the architecture is defined, when developers start feature work, then they have clear API contracts and data models to build against
- Given CI/CD is configured, when code is merged to main, then it automatically builds, tests, and deploys to the dev environment
- Given a user authenticates, when they access the application, then their identity and permissions are validated correctly
- Given the application is running, when operators need to troubleshoot, then logs and traces are available with correlation IDs

## Definition of Done
- Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) documented for key technical choices
- ERD and API contracts reviewed and approved
- CI/CD pipeline successfully deploys to at least one environment
- Authentication flow tested end-to-end
- Observability verified with sample requests showing correlated logs/traces
- Security review completed for infrastructure and auth patterns

## Sizing Guidance
This is typically a **Medium (M)** epic for simple applications or **Large (L)** for enterprise applications with compliance requirements. Consider splitting into sub-epics if the scope exceeds 2-3 sprints:
- Architecture & Data Modeling
- Infrastructure & DevOps
- Authentication & Security
- Observability & Operations

value.title: Implement compliance and regulatory requirements for [Application Name]
value.description:
## Summary
Ensure [Application Name] meets all applicable compliance and regulatory requirements based on the industry and data being handled. This epic addresses legal, security, and privacy obligations.

## Business Outcomes
- Avoid legal/regulatory penalties by meeting compliance requirements before launch
- Build user trust through demonstrated security and privacy practices
- Enable enterprise sales by meeting procurement security requirements

## Scope (Adapt based on domain)
- **Healthcare (HIPAA)**: PHI encryption at rest/transit, access controls, audit logging, BAA requirements
- **Financial (SOC2/PCI)**: Data protection controls, access reviews, penetration testing, audit trails
- **Privacy (GDPR/CCPA)**: Consent management, data subject rights, data retention policies, privacy notices
- **General Security**: Vulnerability management, incident response plan, security training

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)
- Given sensitive data is stored, when at rest, then it is encrypted using approved algorithms
- Given a user requests their data, when the request is processed, then they can export or delete their data within regulatory timeframes
- Given a security incident occurs, when detected, then the incident response plan is executed and documented
- Given an audit is requested, when auditors review logs, then all required events are captured with timestamps and actor IDs

## Definition of Done
- Compliance requirements mapped to technical controls
- Data classification completed for all data types
- Required security controls implemented and tested
- Privacy policy and terms of service reviewed by legal
- Audit logging verified to capture required events
- Penetration test or security assessment completed (if required)

## Sizing Guidance
Compliance epics are typically **Large (L)** or **Extra Large (XL)** for regulated industries. Consider splitting by compliance domain if multiple frameworks apply.
